original_abstract: Integrating the philosophy of Christian Religious Education (CRE) into theological curricula is far from a transgressive endeavor; rather, it is a vital practice that fosters personal growth and the edification of the faith community. Entrusting theology students with the rigors of CRE philosophy is essential, as the discipline cultivates discernment and harmonizes the intellectual, emotional, and spiritual faculties toward a pursuit of true wisdom. Because this philosophical inquiry is intrinsically linked to scriptural doctrine, the Holy Bible serves as the foundational authority and primary standard for all academic study in this field.
humanized_abstract: According to the philosophy of Christian Religious Education (CRE) to incorporate it into the study of theology cannot be described as a transgressive process. Rather it is an educational process which promotes the development of the individual and the church. In fact it is essential to prepare students of theology to manage the CRE philosophy. As CRE philosophy is concerned with discrimination and the unity of the intellect, emotions and spirit in the quest for wisdom, it can be concluded that students must be prepared to engage with these issues. Since CRE philosophy is directly related to biblical doctrine, the Holy Bible is the standard and authoritative text for CRE studies.
